metadata_domains.title: Replay and recovery
metadata_domains.description: Replay steps, recovery ownership, and completion outcomes for persistent metadata changes.

metadata_domains.title: Topology and membership
metadata_domains.description: Device, root, list, pointer, and membership changes that must roll back or transfer ownership.

metadata_domains.title: Return and retry provenance
metadata_domains.description: The returned result must describe the current metadata operation attempt.

metadata_domains.title: Accounting and reservation
metadata_domains.description: Reservations, pending metadata work, counters, and activation obligations.

metadata_domains.title: Metadata transaction lifecycle
metadata_domains.description: Journal or transaction begin, commit, abort, and ownership transfer around metadata updates.

metadata_domains.title: Metadata allocation lifecycle
metadata_domains.description: Allocation, publication, rollback, and release when the object is part of a metadata operation.

metadata_domains.title: Namespace and orphan state
metadata_domains.description: Directory membership, link counts, orphan tracking, and namespace failure completion.

metadata_domains.title: Quota and reference accounting
metadata_domains.description: Quota objects, reference counts, and paired accounting updates for metadata mutations.

metadata_domains.title: Deferred metadata ownership
metadata_domains.description: Incomplete metadata work transferred to recovery, background, or deferred handlers.

inclusion_requirements: The filesystem is one of the four target filesystems.
The operation changes, restores, accounts for, or controls persistent filesystem metadata.
The suspected obligation can be stated as an exposed metadata effect, explicit protection, closure, retry result, or accounting relation.
Object identity and the relevant failure or success exit are explicit enough for conservative analysis.

include_when: The resource is a named protocol object whose lifetime is necessary for a metadata effect or legal exit.
Independent evidence connects the resource lifecycle to metadata consistency, recovery, namespace, quota, or topology state.
exclude_when: The record only proves a generic temporary memory, buffer, folio, path, or filename leak.
No metadata effect, accounting obligation, ownership transfer, or operation completion consequence is established.

confirmed_bug_decisions.rationale: Required ext4 replay step and returned outcome.
Required ext4 replay step and returned outcome.
The ledger establishes a buffer_head leak but no metadata completion or consistency obligation.
The returned error describes a stale metadata-space attempt after retry.
The replay error propagation is in scope; the separate iloc.bh leak is not used as rule evidence.
The ledger establishes a mapping_node leak without an explicit metadata completion obligation.
Relocation-root membership and recovery failure rollback are metadata topology state.
Realtime summary metadata copy must propagate its operation outcome.
The ledger establishes a buffer_head leak; it does not establish a directory metadata completion violation.
The ledger establishes a temporary extent-path leak without an explicit metadata completion obligation.
The ledger establishes a temporary filename buffer leak without an explicit namespace completion violation.
A dquot reference is a quota metadata ownership and accounting object.
Realtime metadata inode loading must propagate failure to the operation exit.
The ledger establishes a dentry folio leak without an explicit namespace completion obligation.
Chunk metadata reservation is an explicit accounting obligation.
Failed device addition leaves metadata topology membership on the transaction update list.
Failed device addition must restore active metadata topology pointers.
Failed device addition must roll back filesystem device topology state.
